question_text: Example block 1. These are single-item Likert questions that can measure baseline attitudes or post-task outcomes.
Example single-item question. Replace this text with one attitude or outcome item that matters in your study.
This item shows that quoted text such as "important instruction" can appear in question wording.
Example block 2. These are grouped Likert questions that share one response scale across several related statements.
This grouped item shows how one statement can appear inside a shared battery of related measures.
Researchers often use grouped Likert items for trust usability workload or other multi-item constructs.
Example block 3. These are open-ended text questions for comments explanations or qualitative reflections.
Briefly describe how you might adapt this baseline template for your own study.
Which study materials would you replace first: page text questions task instructions or hidden prompts?
Example block 4. These are single-select multiple-choice questions with one answer chosen from a fixed set of options.
Which TRI-CA configuration area would you most likely edit first?
Which response format would be most useful in your study?
options: Single Likert item|Grouped Likert block|Open-ended text|A mix of several formats
